jd:
Overview
Splitwise is looking for a Data Scientist to join our growing team. At Splitwise, Data Scientists use data to understand how people send and share money, uncover opportunities to improve our user experience and offer new products, and measure the impact of what we ship. The most important product and business decisions we make at Splitwise have always been informed by what we learn from our users. You'll be helping tens of millions of people manage money with their friends and family and reduce the stress that sharing costs places on relationships.

Splitwise is a large consumer app that handles millions of interactions per day. Using a combination of analytical thinking, statistical rigor, and practical product sense, you'll unpack that data to learn how people use Splitwise and why. You'll turn those insights into clear recommendations for our product, engineering, and business teams, helping them monitor changes, investigate key questions, and make better decisions every week.

Our Data team works at the intersection of product analytics, experimentation, business intelligence, and statistical modeling. You might help shape new features by designing and analyzing A/B tests, defining the metrics we rely on to make decisions, building self-serve dashboards that teams use every day, digging into long-term retention and engagement patterns, and developing advanced models in areas such as trust and safety scores, forecasting, and anomaly detection.

You'll collaborate with other data scientists, as well as our product, engineering, and business teams to help Splitwise scale on our journey to 100 million+ users. We're currently a small team - and you can make a huge difference.

What you'll actually do at Splitwise:
Design, run, and analyze experiments (A/B tests and similar) to measure the impact of new features and product changes
Define, implement, and maintain core product metrics, dashboards and reports that reflect how well we're serving our users
Dive deep into open-ended questions and turn fuzzy problems into structured analyses and clear recommendations
Collaborate closely with product managers, designers, engineers, and business stakeholders to prioritize questions, shape experiment designs, and interpret results
Use SQL and Python to run analyses, prototype models, and build reusable workflows
Communicate your findings to teammates to help them make informed product and technical decisions
Contribute to improving our data tools, processes, and best practices as we scale to many more users and teammates
Things about you:
You enjoy working in a team, and treat others with empathy and respect
You have strong quantitative and analytical skills, a solid understanding of statistics, and the curiosity to dig deeper when needed
You're comfortable writing SQL to query large datasets and dig into crosstabs to answer complex questions
You have the ability to write code in a scripting language (Python, R, etc.) to wrangle data, run analyses, and create useful visualizations
You're able to translate ambiguous product or business questions into concrete analytical tasks, and you care about picking the right metric or method for the problem
You have strong collaboration and communication skills, and can work with product managers, designers, engineers, and business leaders on both technical and strategic topics
You value intellectual honesty and are transparent about uncertainty, limitations, and alternative explanations in your work
You have at least a Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Math or Applied Math, Computer Science, Physics, Economics, Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent experience
Things that are not required but highly valued: past role on a Data or Analytics team; experience at a tech company (especially a consumer tech company) on the Growth, Marketing, or Product teams; advanced statistical expertise; a PhD or Masters in a relevant field; software engineering experience; a cool or impactful data science project you can share with us
Things you'll learn:
How to empower data-hungry teammates to answer their own questions with reliable self-serve tools
How to shape product strategy by turning messy real-world questions into structured analyses and experiments
How to support strategic decisions in consumer social finance using quantitative research
How to build and maintain dashboards that become critical to product and business operations
How to assess product changes through a mix of qualitative and quantitative evidence and research
How to think about trust & safety, fraud, and business operations through a statistical and product lens
How a small, transparent start-up operates
Compensation range:
$130,500 - $184,000/yr base cash compensation
Depends on experience level and tenure
Benefits:
Competitive base salary
Equity/stock options
Top-tier health care (covered 100% for you, 50% for dependents)
Dental and Vision insurance
Unlimited/flexible PTO (most employees take 4-6 weeks)
Paid parental leave (12+ weeks)
401k with match
Flexible hours (with meeting hours generally from 10am-5pm ET)
Professional development budget for conferences or other paid resources, in consultation with your manager
Periodic annual retreats and face-to-face visits with colleagues
Support for in-office, co-working, or remote work, each with related benefits to do your best work
Application Guidelines
Email jobs@splitwise.com from your personal email (not work email). Include your resume and optionally attach additional files or links (portfolio, LinkedIn, etc.).

Please include an attached resume and concise (1-4 sentences) answers to the following questions in your application email:

Splitwise is headquartered in Providence, RI and has a strong presence in New York, NY. We also support hybrid arrangements with regular visits to these locations, as well as fully remote roles within the USA. Which of these are you interested in?
What interests you about working with Splitwise specifically?
Data Science as a title can mean many different things at many different companies. Some distinct areas of focus are (A) business intelligence reporting, dashboards, and forecasting (B) exploratory product analytics investigating product-related topics, (C) statistical inference and designing product experiments, (D) algorithms, machine learning, and statistics-based product features, and (E) generative AI and LLMs. Please rank these areas of focus in order of personal interest from most interested (1) to least interested (5).
In a few brief sentences, describe an interesting piece of data analysis work you’ve done in the past, and summarize the most important conclusion or impact from that work. (You may optionally also share a link to a summary, journal article, blog post or code for this analysis, if one is available.)
Consider a mobile app that you often use and are familiar with (ex: Gmail on Android). Briefly propose a product change you believe could be valuable for that product, and explain how you would test if that product change was effective.
Splitwise is an equal opportunity employer. We value diversity and encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply.
